pattern: Padding the string with a sentinel active cell on each side turns every inactive stretch into something bounded by active neighbors, so the one useful trade is always: erase some isolated block of active cells and let its two neighboring inactive stretches merge and flip on -- the best gain is just the largest sum of two neighboring inactive-run lengths around any interior active run.
hints: Pad the string with an extra active cell on both ends first (conceptually); then split the padded string into maximal runs of the same character.

Only an active run that has an inactive run immediately before AND after it (i.e. not the very first or very last run) can ever be the block you erase -- erasing it merges its two neighboring inactive runs into one, which then flips fully active.

For every such interior active run, the trade's payoff equals the combined length of the inactive run just before it plus the inactive run just after it; the answer is the string's original active-cell count plus the best payoff found (or just the original count if no such run exists).

edge_inputs.description: statement example, no interior block to trade
statement example, a single isolated active cell between two inactive runs
statement example, active cells at both ends with an interior block
statement example, alternating cells with two candidate interior blocks
single inactive cell, no trade possible
entirely inactive, no active runs exist at all
entirely active already, a trade could only ever break it
two isolated active cells flanked by unequal inactive runs, so the larger payoff is not the first candidate encountered

stress.description: random binary strings up to the maximum length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(100000, size))
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('01') for _ in range(n))
    return [s]
